topics.title: What is Python?
topics.content:
What is Python?

Python is a high-level, interpreted programming language created by Guido van Rossum in 1991. It is used in web development, AI, data science, automation, and game development.

Why Python?

Python reads almost like plain English — making it one of the best first languages.

# Python — clean and simple
print("Hello, World!")

topics.title: Your First Program
topics.content:
Hello, World!

print("Hello, World!")

The print() function outputs text to the screen.

Comments

# This is a comment
print("This runs!")  # inline comment

topics.title: Python Syntax Basics
topics.content:
Indentation

Python uses indentation to define code blocks — this is mandatory.

if True:
    print("Indented correctly")

Case Sensitivity

name = "Alice"
Name = "Bob"
print(name)  # Alice
print(Name)  # Bob

evalQuestions.question: Which function displays output in Python?
What symbol writes a comment in Python?
Python was created by ___.
What does `print(2 + 3)` output?

topics.title: Variables
topics.content:
What is a Variable?

A variable is a named container that stores a value.

name = "Alice"      # string
age  = 20           # integer
height = 5.6        # float
is_student = True   # boolean

Naming Rules

  • Start with a letter or underscore
  • No reserved keywords
  • Case-sensitive

topics.title: Data Types
topics.content:
Core Data Types

  • int — whole numbers: 5, -3
  • float — decimals: 3.14
  • str — text: "hello"
  • bool — True or False
print(type(42))     # <class 'int'>
print(type(3.14))   # <class 'float'>
print(type("hi"))   # <class 'str'>

topics.title: Type Conversion
topics.content:
Converting Types

x = "42"
y = int(x)     # → 42
z = float(x)   # → 42.0
w = str(100)   # → "100"

print(y + 8)       # 50
print(w + " pts")  # "100 pts"

evalQuestions.question: Which type stores 3.14?
What does `type(True)` return?
What will `int("7") + 3` evaluate to?
What function converts a value to a string?

topics.title: Arithmetic Operators
topics.content:
Arithmetic Operators

a = 10; b = 3
print(a + b)   # 13
print(a - b)   # 7
print(a * b)   # 30
print(a / b)   # 3.333
print(a // b)  # 3  floor division
print(a % b)   # 1  remainder
print(a ** b)  # 1000 exponent

topics.title: Comparison & Logical
topics.content:
Comparison Operators

x = 10; y = 5
print(x == y)  # False
print(x != y)  # True
print(x > y)   # True
print(x >= 10) # True

Logical Operators

print(True and False)  # False
print(True or False)   # True
print(not True)        # False

topics.title: Assignment Operators
topics.content:
Shorthand Assignment

score = 100
score += 10   # 110
score -= 5    # 105
score *= 2    # 210
score //= 3   # 70
print(score)

evalQuestions.question: What does `10 % 3` evaluate to?
What is `2 ** 4`?
What does `x += 5` mean?
Which operator checks equality?

topics.title: if / else
topics.content:
Making Decisions

score = 65

if score >= 70:
    print("You passed!")
else:
    print("Keep practicing.")

topics.title: elif — Multiple Conditions
topics.content:
elif

grade = 85

if grade >= 90:
    print("A — Excellent!")
elif grade >= 80:
    print("B — Great job!")
elif grade >= 70:
    print("C — Good work.")
else:
    print("Needs improvement.")

topics.title: Ternary Expression
topics.content:
One-Line if

result = "Pass" if score >= 70 else "Fail"
print(result)

evalQuestions.question: What keyword checks a secondary condition after `if`?
What will `x = 10; print("big") if x > 5 else print("small")` output?
In Python, indentation inside an if block is ___.

topics.title: while Loop
topics.content:
while Loop

count = 1
while count <= 5:
    print(count)
    count += 1
# Prints 1 2 3 4 5

break and continue

i = 0
while True:
    i += 1
    if i == 3: continue
    if i > 5:  break
    print(i)  # 1 2 4 5

topics.title: for Loop & range()
topics.content:
for Loop

for i in range(5):
    print(i)  # 0 1 2 3 4

fruits = ["apple","banana","cherry"]
for fruit in fruits:
    print(fruit)

range() Variants

range(5)         # 0-4
range(1, 6)      # 1-5
range(0, 10, 2)  # 0 2 4 6 8

evalQuestions.question: How many times does `for i in range(3)` loop?
What keyword immediately exits a loop?
What does `range(2, 8, 2)` produce?
Which keyword skips the current iteration?

topics.title: Defining Functions
topics.content:
Functions

def greet(name):
    print(f"Hello, {name}!")

greet("Alice")  # Hello, Alice!

topics.title: Return Values & Defaults
topics.content:
Return

def add(a, b):
    return a + b

print(add(5, 3))  # 8

Default Parameters

def power(base, exponent=2):
    return base ** exponent

print(power(3))    # 9
print(power(3, 3)) # 27

topics.title: Scope
topics.content:
Local vs Global

counter = 0

def increment():
    global counter
    counter += 1

increment()
increment()
print(counter)  # 2

evalQuestions.question: What keyword defines a function?
What does `double(6)` return if `def double(x): return x * 2`?
A variable created inside a function is called ___.
What keyword sends a value back from a function?

topics.title: Lists
topics.content:
Lists

fruits = ["apple","banana","cherry"]
print(fruits[0])    # apple
print(fruits[-1])   # cherry

fruits.append("mango")
fruits.remove("banana")
print(len(fruits))

topics.title: Tuples
topics.content:
Tuples — Immutable

point = (3, 7)
print(point[0])  # 3
# point[0] = 10  # TypeError!

x, y = (10, 20)  # unpacking
print(x, y)

evalQuestions.question: Which method adds to the end of a list?
What index is the first element?
What makes a tuple different from a list?

topics.title: Dictionaries
topics.content:
Key-Value Pairs

student = {"name":"Alice","age":20,"grade":"A"}

print(student["name"])     # Alice
student["score"] = 95      # add
del student["age"]         # remove

for k, v in student.items():
    print(f"{k}: {v}")

topics.title: Sets
topics.content:
Sets — Unique Values

colors = {"red","green","blue","red"}
print(colors)  # no duplicate!

a = {1,2,3,4}; b = {3,4,5,6}
print(a | b)  # Union
print(a & b)  # Intersection {3,4}
print(a - b)  # Difference  {1,2}

evalQuestions.question: How do you access key "name" in dict `d`?
What does a set guarantee?
Which method returns all key-value pairs as tuples?

topics.title: Common String Methods
topics.content:
String Methods

text = "  Hello, Python!  "
print(text.strip())          # remove spaces
print(text.lower())          # lowercase
print(text.upper())          # UPPERCASE
print(text.replace("Python","World"))
words = "the quick fox".split()
print("-".join(words))       # the-quick-fox

topics.title: f-strings & Slicing
topics.content:
f-strings

name  = "Alice"
score = 95.5
print(f"Hello, {name}!")
print(f"Score: {score:.1f}%")

Slicing

text = "Hello, World!"
print(text[0:5])    # Hello
print(text[::-1])   # reversed

evalQuestions.question: What does `"hello".upper()` return?
Which f-string embeds variable `x = 5` correctly?
What does `text[::-1]` do?

topics.title: Reading & Writing Files
topics.content:
File I/O

# Read
with open("data.txt", "r") as f:
    print(f.read())

# Write
with open("out.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write("Hello!\n")

# Append
with open("out.txt", "a") as f:
    f.write("More content\n")

💡 Always use with — it auto-closes the file.

evalQuestions.question: Which mode appends without overwriting?
What is the advantage of `with open(...) as f`?
Which mode overwrites a file completely?

topics.title: Classes & Objects
topics.content:
Classes

class Dog:
    def __init__(self, name, age):
        self.name = name
        self.age  = age
    def bark(self):
        return f"{self.name}: Woof!"

dog = Dog("Rex", 3)
print(dog.bark())   # Rex: Woof!

topics.title: Inheritance
topics.content:
Inheritance

class Animal:
    def __init__(self, name):
        self.name = name
    def speak(self): return "..."

class Cat(Animal):
    def speak(self):
        return f"{self.name}: Meow!"

print(Cat("Whiskers").speak())

evalQuestions.question: What special method initializes a new object?
In `class Cat(Animal):`, what is Animal?
What does `self` refer to in a method?

topics.title: try / except / finally
topics.content:
Error Handling

try:
    n = int(input("Enter number: "))
    print(10 / n)
except ValueError:
    print("Not a number!")
except ZeroDivisionError:
    print("Cannot divide by zero!")
finally:
    print("Always runs.")

evalQuestions.question: Which block always executes?
What exception is raised on division by zero?
What keyword deliberately triggers an exception?

topics.title: Comprehensions
topics.content:
List Comprehensions

squares = [x**2 for x in range(1,6)]
print(squares)  # [1,4,9,16,25]

evens = [x for x in range(10) if x % 2 == 0]
print(evens)    # [0,2,4,6,8]

topics.title: Lambda & map/filter
topics.content:
Lambda

double = lambda x: x * 2
print(double(5))  # 10

nums = [1,2,3,4,5,6]
doubled = list(map(lambda x: x*2, nums))
evens   = list(filter(lambda x: x%2==0, nums))
print(doubled)  # [2,4,6,8,10,12]
print(evens)    # [2,4,6]

evalQuestions.question: What does `[x**2 for x in range(4)]` produce?
What does `lambda x: x * 3` do?
options: Creates an anonymous function that triples x
evalQuestions.correctAnswer: Creates an anonymous function that triples x

evalQuestions.question: Which built-in applies a function to every element?

topics.title: What is JavaScript?
topics.content:
What is JavaScript?

JavaScript (JS) is the language of the web. It runs in every browser and makes pages interactive. With Node.js it also runs on servers.

console.log("Hello, World!");
console.log(2 + 3);  // 5

topics.title: Variables — let, const, var
topics.content:
Declaring Variables

let   name  = "Alice";  // reassignable
const PI    = 3.14;     // constant
var   age   = 20;       // old-style, avoid

let score = 85;
score = 90;  // OK
// PI = 4;  // TypeError!

💡 Use const by default, let when you need to change the value.

topics.title: Data Types
topics.content:
JS Data Types

let num     = 42;        // Number
let price   = 9.99;      // Number
let name    = "Alice";   // String
let isReady = true;      // Boolean
let empty   = null;      // Null
let undef;               // Undefined

console.log(typeof num);   // "number"
console.log(typeof name);  // "string"

evalQuestions.question: Which function logs output to the console?
Which keyword declares a constant (cannot be reassigned)?
What does `typeof "hello"` return?
What does `"5" + 3` evaluate to?

topics.title: Arithmetic & Assignment
topics.content:
Arithmetic

let a = 10, b = 3;
console.log(a + b);   // 13
console.log(a % b);   // 1
console.log(a ** b);  // 1000

let x = 10;
x += 5;  // 15
x++;     // 16

topics.title: Comparison & Logical
topics.content:
Comparison

console.log(5 == "5");   // true  (loose)
console.log(5 === "5");  // false (strict)
console.log(5 !== 3);    // true

💡 Always use === in JS.

Logical

console.log(true && false); // false
console.log(true || false); // true
console.log(!true);         // false

topics.title: Template Literals
topics.content:
Template Literals

const name  = "Alice";
const score = 95;
console.log(`Hello, ${name}!`);
console.log(`Score: ${score}%`);
console.log(`2 + 2 = ${2 + 2}`);

evalQuestions.question: What does `10 % 3` evaluate to?
Which operator checks both value AND type?
What syntax embeds expressions in template literals?
`x++` is shorthand for ___.

topics.title: if / else / switch
topics.content:
if / else if / else

const grade = 85;
if (grade >= 90)      { console.log("A"); }
else if (grade >= 80) { console.log("B"); }
else                  { console.log("C"); }

switch

switch (day) {
    case "Monday": console.log("Start!"); break;
    case "Friday": console.log("TGIF!"); break;
    default:       console.log("Midweek");
}

topics.title: Loops
topics.content:
for Loop

for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
    console.log(i);
}

const fruits = ["apple","banana","cherry"];
for (const fruit of fruits) {
    console.log(fruit);
}

while Loop

let n = 1;
while (n <= 5) {
    console.log(n);
    n++;
}

evalQuestions.question: What prevents fall-through in a switch statement?
Which loop iterates over array items using `of`?
In `for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++)`, how many iterations?

topics.title: Declaration & Expression
topics.content:
Function Declaration

function greet(name) {
    return `Hello, ${name}!`;
}
console.log(greet("Alice"));

Default Parameters

function power(base, exp = 2) {
    return base ** exp;
}
console.log(power(3));    // 9
console.log(power(3,3));  // 27

topics.title: Arrow Functions
topics.content:
Arrow Syntax

const double = x => x * 2;
console.log(double(5));  // 10

const add = (a, b) => a + b;
console.log(add(3, 5));  // 8

// With array methods:
const nums = [1,2,3,4,5];
const squared = nums.map(n => n ** 2);
console.log(squared); // [1,4,9,16,25]

evalQuestions.question: Which is a valid arrow function?
What does `const add = (a,b) => a+b; add(4,6)` return?
What keyword returns a value from a function?

topics.title: Array Methods
topics.content:
map / filter / reduce

const nums = [1,2,3,4,5];

const doubled = nums.map(n => n * 2);
console.log(doubled);   // [2,4,6,8,10]

const evens = nums.filter(n => n % 2 === 0);
console.log(evens);     // [2,4]

const sum = nums.reduce((acc, n) => acc + n, 0);
console.log(sum);       // 15

topics.title: Spread & Destructuring
topics.content:
Destructuring

const [first, second, ...rest] = [10,20,30,40];
console.log(first);  // 10
console.log(rest);   // [30,40]

Spread

const a = [1,2,3], b = [4,5,6];
const combined = [...a, ...b];
console.log(combined); // [1,2,3,4,5,6]

evalQuestions.question: Which method transforms each element into a new array?
What does `[1,2,3].filter(n => n > 1)` return?
What does `[...a, ...b]` do?

topics.title: Object Literals
topics.content:
Objects

const student = {
    name: "Alice", age: 20,
    greet() { return `Hi, I'm ${this.name}`; }
};
console.log(student.name);    // Alice
console.log(student.greet()); // Hi, I'm Alice
student.score = 95;  // add property

topics.title: Destructuring & Spread
topics.content:
Object Destructuring

const { name, age } = student;
console.log(name);  // Alice

// Rename
const { name: fullName } = student;

Spread

const base = { theme:"dark", lang:"en" };
const user = { ...base, name:"Alice" };
console.log(user);

evalQuestions.question: What does `this` refer to inside an object method?
What does `const { name } = person` do?
How do you add property `score = 100` to object `obj`?

topics.title: Selecting & Modifying Elements
topics.content:
Selecting Elements

const title = document.getElementById("main-title");
const btn   = document.querySelector(".submit-btn");
const items = document.querySelectorAll("li");

title.textContent = "New Title";
title.style.color = "red";
btn.classList.add("active");

topics.title: Event Listeners
topics.content:
Events

const btn = document.querySelector("#myBtn");

btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    btn.textContent = "Clicked!";
    btn.style.background = "green";
});

document.addEventListener("keydown", (e) => {
    console.log("Key:", e.key);
});

evalQuestions.question: Which method selects the first element matching a CSS selector?
Which method attaches an event handler?
What property sets visible text without parsing HTML?

topics.title: Promises
topics.content:
Promises

const p = new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    setTimeout(() => resolve("Done!"), 1000);
});

p.then(data  => console.log(data))
 .catch(err  => console.error(err))
 .finally(() => console.log("Always"));

topics.title: async / await & Fetch
topics.content:
async / await

async function fetchUser(id) {
    try {
        const res  = await fetch(`/api/users/${id}`);
        const data = await res.json();
        console.log(data.name);
    } catch (err) {
        console.error("Failed:", err);
    }
}
fetchUser(1);

evalQuestions.question: Which keyword pauses until a Promise resolves?
What does `.catch()` handle?
A function using `await` must be declared with ___.

topics.title: Classes & Inheritance
topics.content:
Classes

class Animal {
    constructor(name) { this.name = name; }
    speak() { return `${this.name} makes a sound.`; }
}

class Dog extends Animal {
    speak() { return `${this.name} barks.`; }
}

const d = new Dog("Rex");
console.log(d.speak());           // Rex barks.
console.log(d instanceof Animal); // true

topics.title: Modules (import / export)
topics.content:
ES6 Modules

// math.js
export function add(a,b) { return a+b; }
export const PI = 3.14;
export default function mul(a,b) { return a*b; }
// main.js
import mul from "./math.js";
import { add, PI } from "./math.js";

console.log(add(3,5));  // 8
console.log(mul(4,5));  // 20

evalQuestions.question: Which keyword inherits from a parent class?
Which syntax imports the default export?
What is `constructor()` used for?

topics.title: try / catch / finally
topics.content:
Error Handling

try {
    const data = JSON.parse(badJson);
} catch (err) {
    console.error("Failed:", err.message);
} finally {
    console.log("Cleanup done.");
}

function divide(a,b) {
    if (b === 0) throw new Error("Division by zero!");
    return a / b;
}

topics.title: Closures
topics.content:
Closures

A function that remembers its outer scope variables.

function makeCounter() {
    let count = 0;
    return function() { return ++count; };
}

const counter = makeCounter();
console.log(counter());  // 1
console.log(counter());  // 2
console.log(counter());  // 3

evalQuestions.question: Which block always runs?
What is a closure?
options: A function that remembers outer scope variables
A way to close a file
evalQuestions.correctAnswer: A function that remembers outer scope variables

evalQuestions.question: Which keyword throws an exception?
